  "findings": [
    "Bearing damaged during installation due to pressing force transmitted through the rolling elements (wrong race pressed)",
    "Premature bearing failure, noise, and vibration during in-service operation"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Shut down the machinery immediately to prevent catastrophic shaft or housing damage",
    "Remove the damaged bearing assembly",
    "Install a new bearing using correct mounting tools (e.g., impact ring pressing only on the interference-fitted race) in accordance with SKF/Timken guides"
  ],
  "rationale": "Pressing a bearing onto a shaft or into a housing by applying force to the wrong race transmits the mounting force through the rolling elements. This causes immediate brinelling (denting) of the raceways, leading to rapid degradation, severe vibration, and premature failure once the asset is in service.",
  "references": [
    "SKF Bearing Installation Guide (Mounting forces)",
    "Timken Engineering Manual (Proper mounting practices)"
  ]
}
